name: Live activity noteworthiness threshold audit

scope.question: Do all current live /activity records clear the new field-activity noteworthiness threshold and remain consistent with activity/evidence boundary guidance?

summary: Audited the eight live /activity records against the new field_anchor/material_program threshold. All current items clear the public threshold after explicit threshold metadata was added. Calico launch is a field_anchor. Calico-AbbVie, Calico-Broad, TPN-101 ARPA-H funding, elamipretide SHAPE recruiting, autologous mitochondrial transplantation human trial launches, and sirolimus older-adult trial-watch context are material_program items. The sirolimus item is the closest call: it should be kept because it is a direct older-adult rapalog trial-watch item in a visible track, not because a routine registry-update date is inherently notable.

materiality_decision.rationale: The audit changed internal noteworthiness metadata and validation policy but did not identify a live public activity item that should be removed or reclassified below the public threshold.

search_log.summary: Reviewed every JSON file under data/activity-items for event type, source anchoring, scope label, track linkage, and activity/evidence boundary copy.

search_log.source_name: Sirolimus older-adults local source and study records
search_log.summary: Confirmed the sirolimus item remains recruiting no-results trial-watch context for a direct older-adult rapalog trial. It clears the threshold as direct track-relevant clinical-transition activity, not as routine registry churn.

notes: Added noteworthiness_tier and threshold_basis to each live activity item.
Added validation so future live activity items must be field_anchor or material_program and must include threshold_basis.
No live item was moved to watch_only or below_threshold during this audit.
next_actions: When the next field-activity packet is presented to the human, include threshold tier and threshold basis for every candidate.
If future registry-only items are routine metadata checks rather than direct trial-watch anchors, keep them in research sessions or trial records instead of /activity.
